---
title: Project Description
author:
  - Solveig Røndal-Liniger (szc836)
  - Oliver Eiberg Jørgensen (zkt394)
date: 2026-04-23
date-format: MMMM DD, YYYY
jupyter: python3
execute: 
  echo: false
  warning: false
format:
  pdf: 
    number-sections: true
    colorlinks: true
    geometry:
      - top=30mm
      - left=25mm
      - bottom=25mm
      - right=25mm
      - includefoot=false
    lang: en
    fontsize: 12pt
    highlight-style: github-light
    include-in-header:
      text: |
        \usepackage{setspace}
        \usepackage{booktabs}
        \usepackage{float}
        \setstretch{1.3}
        \setlength{\floatsep}{6pt}
        \setlength{\textfloatsep}{6pt}
        \setlength{\intextsep}{6pt}
        \setlength{\abovecaptionskip}{4pt}
        \setlength{\belowcaptionskip}{2pt}
        \RedeclareSectionCommand[beforeskip=0.1ex,afterskip=0.01ex]{section}
        \RedeclareSectionCommand[beforeskip=0.1ex,afterskip=0.01ex]{subsection}
        \RedeclareSectionCommand[beforeskip=0.1ex,afterskip=0.01ex]{subsubsection}
        \usepackage{fancyhdr}
        \usepackage{graphicx}
        \usepackage{eso-pic}
        \pagestyle{fancy}
        \setlength{\footskip}{36pt}
        \fancyhf{}
        \lfoot{\shortstack[l]{{\bfseries University of Copenhagen}\\Department of Economics}}
        \rfoot{\thepage}
        \renewcommand{\headrulewidth}{0pt}
        \renewcommand{\footrulewidth}{0pt}
        \fancypagestyle{plain}{%
          \fancyhf{}
          \lfoot{\shortstack[l]{{\bfseries University of Copenhagen}\\Department of Economics}}
          \rfoot{\thepage}
          \renewcommand{\headrulewidth}{0pt}
          \renewcommand{\footrulewidth}{0pt}
        }
        \AddToShipoutPictureFG{%
          \AtPageUpperLeft{%
            \put(\LenToUnit{\paperwidth-10mm-5cm},\LenToUnit{-2.5cm}){%
              \includegraphics[width=5cm]{ku_logo_uk_h.png}%
            }%
          }%
        }
---

<!-- Importing libraries -->
```{python}
# Loading necessary libraries
import pandas as pd
import numpy as np
from scipy.optimize import minimize
from scipy.special import expit
from scipy.stats import chi2, norm
from pathlib import Path
#import os

# Path to the Excel file containing the data
#PROJECT_ROOT = Path(os.environ.get("QUARTO_PROJECT_DIR", Path.cwd())).resolve()
#DATA_PATH = PROJECT_ROOT / "Data"
#SCANNER_DATA = DATA_PATH / "panel_with_state.csv"
SCANNER_DATA = "/Users/olivereiberg/Documents/Economics/MA/2_Semester/Data/panel_with_state.csv"
PRICE_PROMO_DATA = "/Users/olivereiberg/Documents/Economics/MA/2_Semester/Data/Price_Promo.xlsx"
NETTO_DF_EXPORT_PATH = Path("/Users/olivereiberg/Documents/Economics/MA/2_Semester/Data/netto_df.csv")

# Set seed for reproducibility.
SEED = 1800
rng = np.random.default_rng(SEED) # rng is the explicit random-number generator used everywhere below instead of relying on NumPy's global random state.
```
\newpage

```{python}
EMPIRICAL_BRANDS = [1, 2]
EMPIRICAL_PRICE_COLUMNS = {
    1: "Brand_1_price",
    2: "Brand_2_price",
}

# Load scanner panel
netto_df = pd.read_csv(SCANNER_DATA)

# Match current QMD setup
netto_df = netto_df[netto_df["week_num"] != 53].copy()

netto_df["Brand"] = netto_df["Brand"].astype(str)
netto_df["Price"] = pd.to_numeric(netto_df["Price"], errors="coerce").round(1)
netto_df["Customer_ID"] = pd.to_numeric(netto_df["Customer_ID"], errors="coerce").astype("Int64")
netto_df["week_num"] = pd.to_numeric(netto_df["week_num"], errors="coerce").astype("Int64")
netto_df["brand_id"] = pd.to_numeric(netto_df["Brand"], errors="coerce").fillna(0).astype(int)

# Load third Excel sheet with RRP/discount/promo data
price_promo_workbook = pd.ExcelFile(PRICE_PROMO_DATA)
price_promo_sheet_name = price_promo_workbook.sheet_names[2]
price_promo_df = pd.read_excel(price_promo_workbook, sheet_name=price_promo_sheet_name)

price_promo_required_columns = [
    "WeekNum",
    "RRP_Brand_1",
    "Discount_Brand_1",
    "Promo_Brand_1",
    "RRP_Brand_2",
    "Discount_Brand_2",
    "Promo_Brand_2",
]

price_promo_df = price_promo_df[price_promo_required_columns].copy()

for column in price_promo_required_columns:
    price_promo_df[column] = pd.to_numeric(price_promo_df[column], errors="coerce")

price_promo_df = price_promo_df.dropna(subset=["WeekNum"]).copy()
price_promo_df["WeekNum"] = price_promo_df["WeekNum"].astype(int)

# Construct offered prices: discount if promo, otherwise RRP
for brand in EMPIRICAL_BRANDS:
    promo_column = f"Promo_Brand_{brand}"
    discount_column = f"Discount_Brand_{brand}"
    rrp_column = f"RRP_Brand_{brand}"
    price_column = EMPIRICAL_PRICE_COLUMNS[brand]

    price_promo_df[promo_column] = price_promo_df[promo_column].fillna(0).astype(int)
    price_promo_df[price_column] = np.where(
        (price_promo_df[promo_column] == 1) & price_promo_df[discount_column].notna(),
        price_promo_df[discount_column],
        price_promo_df[rrp_column],
    )

price_promo_merge_columns = [
    "WeekNum",
    "RRP_Brand_1",
    "Discount_Brand_1",
    "Promo_Brand_1",
    "Brand_1_price",
    "RRP_Brand_2",
    "Discount_Brand_2",
    "Promo_Brand_2",
    "Brand_2_price",
]

# Join weekly price/promo data onto scanner panel
netto_df = netto_df.merge(
    price_promo_df[price_promo_merge_columns],
    left_on="week_num",
    right_on="WeekNum",
    how="left",
    validate="many_to_one",
)

# Keep only rows with both alternative prices available
netto_df = netto_df.loc[
    netto_df["Brand_1_price"].notna() & netto_df["Brand_2_price"].notna()
].copy()

netto_df.head()
```

## Simple Dynamic Empirical Model {.unnumbered}

```{python}
#| output: false

#NETTO_DF_EXPORT_PATH.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
netto_df.to_csv(NETTO_DF_EXPORT_PATH, index=False)
```

The original CML estimator is quite demanding because it only uses very specific purchase histories with matching future price states. As a simpler empirical alternative, we estimate a finite-horizon dynamic logit model directly on the scanner panel. The model keeps all weeks 1--52, but compresses the state space by capping the number of weeks since the household's last purchase at 12.

The household chooses among no purchase, brand 1, and brand 2. The state is the current week, the household's last purchased brand, and the number of weeks since that purchase. Prices and promotions are treated as observed weekly state variables. The dynamic component is estimated by nested fixed point: for each parameter vector, the value function is solved by backward induction and the resulting conditional choice probabilities are used in the likelihood.

```{python}
#| output: asis

SIMPLE_D_CAP = 12
SIMPLE_DELTA = 0.95
SIMPLE_HOUSEHOLD_LIMIT = None
SIMPLE_OPTIMIZER_MAXITER = 500


def print_latex_table(df, caption, label, float_format="%.3f", escape=True):
    """Print a pandas dataframe as a LaTeX table for Quarto/PDF output."""
    latex = df.to_latex(
        index=False,
        escape=escape,
        caption=caption,
        label=label,
        float_format=float_format,
    )
    print(latex)


def logsumexp_np(values, axis=-1):
    max_values = np.max(values, axis=axis, keepdims=True)
    stable_values = values - max_values
    return np.squeeze(
        max_values + np.log(np.exp(stable_values).sum(axis=axis, keepdims=True)),
        axis=axis,
    )


def prepare_simple_model_inputs(data, d_cap=SIMPLE_D_CAP, household_limit=None):
    panel = data.sort_values(["Customer_ID", "week_num"]).copy()

    if household_limit is not None:
        selected_households = panel["Customer_ID"].drop_duplicates().head(household_limit)
        panel = panel.loc[panel["Customer_ID"].isin(selected_households)].copy()

    panel["simple_choice"] = np.where(
        panel["brand_id"].isin(EMPIRICAL_BRANDS),
        panel["brand_id"],
        0,
    ).astype(int)

    panel["simple_purchase_brand"] = panel["simple_choice"].where(panel["simple_choice"] > 0)
    panel["simple_purchase_week"] = panel["week_num"].where(panel["simple_choice"] > 0)

    household_groups = panel.groupby("Customer_ID", sort=False)
    panel["last_purchase_brand"] = household_groups["simple_purchase_brand"].ffill()
    panel["last_purchase_week"] = household_groups["simple_purchase_week"].ffill()

    panel["pre_last_brand"] = household_groups["last_purchase_brand"].shift(1)
    panel["pre_last_purchase_week"] = household_groups["last_purchase_week"].shift(1)

    panel["simple_last_brand"] = (
        panel["pre_last_brand"]
        .fillna(0)
        .astype(int)
        .clip(lower=0, upper=2)
    )

    raw_duration = panel["week_num"] - panel["pre_last_purchase_week"]
    panel["simple_duration"] = np.where(
        panel["simple_last_brand"] == 0,
        0,
        raw_duration.fillna(0),
    )
    panel["simple_duration"] = (
        panel["simple_duration"]
        .clip(lower=0, upper=d_cap)
        .astype(int)
    )

    weekly_prices = (
        panel[["week_num", "Brand_1_price", "Brand_2_price"]]
        .drop_duplicates()
        .sort_values("week_num")
        .reset_index(drop=True)
    )
    week_to_idx = {
        week: idx
        for idx, week in enumerate(weekly_prices["week_num"].to_numpy())
    }
    panel["simple_week_idx"] = panel["week_num"].map(week_to_idx).astype(int)

    price_matrix = weekly_prices[["Brand_1_price", "Brand_2_price"]].to_numpy(dtype=float)

    obs = {
        "week_idx": panel["simple_week_idx"].to_numpy(dtype=int),
        "last_brand": panel["simple_last_brand"].to_numpy(dtype=int),
        "duration": panel["simple_duration"].to_numpy(dtype=int),
        "choice": panel["simple_choice"].to_numpy(dtype=int),
    }

    return panel, weekly_prices, price_matrix, obs


simple_panel, simple_weekly_prices, simple_price_matrix, simple_obs = prepare_simple_model_inputs(
    netto_df,
    d_cap=SIMPLE_D_CAP,
    household_limit=SIMPLE_HOUSEHOLD_LIMIT,
)

simple_sample_summary = pd.DataFrame(
    {
        "Statistic": [
            "Households",
            "Weeks",
            "Household-weeks",
            "Observed purchases",
            "Purchase rate",
            "Brand 1 purchases",
            "Brand 2 purchases",
            "Duration cap",
            "Discount factor",
        ],
        "Value": [
            f"{simple_panel['Customer_ID'].nunique():,}",
            f"{simple_panel['week_num'].nunique():,}",
            f"{len(simple_panel):,}",
            f"{(simple_panel['simple_choice'] > 0).sum():,}",
            f"{(simple_panel['simple_choice'] > 0).mean():.1%}",
            f"{(simple_panel['simple_choice'] == 1).sum():,}",
            f"{(simple_panel['simple_choice'] == 2).sum():,}",
            SIMPLE_D_CAP,
            SIMPLE_DELTA,
        ],
    }
)

print_latex_table(
    simple_sample_summary,
    caption="Sample used for the simple dynamic empirical model.",
    label="tbl:simple-dynamic-sample",
    escape=True,
)
```

```{python}
#| output: asis

def unpack_simple_params(theta_raw):
    return {
        "no_purchase_const": theta_raw[0],
        "brand2_const": theta_raw[1],
        "price_coef": -np.exp(theta_raw[2]),
        "switch_cost": np.exp(theta_raw[3]),
        "duration_penalty": np.exp(theta_raw[4]),
    }


def solve_simple_dynamic_logit(theta_raw, price_matrix, d_cap=SIMPLE_D_CAP, delta=SIMPLE_DELTA):
    params = unpack_simple_params(theta_raw)
    n_weeks = price_matrix.shape[0]
    n_brands_with_outside = 3

    value = np.zeros((n_weeks + 1, n_brands_with_outside, d_cap + 1))
    choice_probs = np.zeros((n_weeks, n_brands_with_outside, d_cap + 1, 3))

    last_brand_grid = np.arange(n_brands_with_outside)[:, None]
    duration_grid = np.arange(d_cap + 1)[None, :]
    has_previous_purchase = last_brand_grid > 0

    no_purchase_next_last = np.repeat(np.arange(n_brands_with_outside)[:, None], d_cap + 1, axis=1)
    no_purchase_next_duration = np.where(
        no_purchase_next_last == 0,
        0,
        np.minimum(duration_grid + 1, d_cap),
    )

    for week_idx in range(n_weeks - 1, -1, -1):
        brand_1_price, brand_2_price = price_matrix[week_idx]

        utility_no_purchase = (
            params["no_purchase_const"]
            - params["duration_penalty"] * duration_grid * has_previous_purchase
        )
        utility_brand_1 = (
            params["price_coef"] * brand_1_price
            - params["switch_cost"] * (last_brand_grid == 2)
            + np.zeros_like(duration_grid, dtype=float)
        )
        utility_brand_2 = (
            params["brand2_const"]
            + params["price_coef"] * brand_2_price
            - params["switch_cost"] * (last_brand_grid == 1)
            + np.zeros_like(duration_grid, dtype=float)
        )

        choice_value = np.stack(
            [utility_no_purchase, utility_brand_1, utility_brand_2],
            axis=2,
        )

        choice_value[:, :, 0] += (
            delta * value[week_idx + 1, no_purchase_next_last, no_purchase_next_duration]
        )
        choice_value[:, :, 1] += delta * value[week_idx + 1, 1, 0]
        choice_value[:, :, 2] += delta * value[week_idx + 1, 2, 0]

        inclusive_value = logsumexp_np(choice_value, axis=2)
        value[week_idx] = inclusive_value
        choice_probs[week_idx] = np.exp(choice_value - inclusive_value[:, :, None])

    return value, choice_probs


def simple_neg_loglik_sum(theta_raw, obs, price_matrix, d_cap=SIMPLE_D_CAP, delta=SIMPLE_DELTA):
    _, choice_probs = solve_simple_dynamic_logit(
        theta_raw,
        price_matrix,
        d_cap=d_cap,
        delta=delta,
    )
    observed_probs = choice_probs[
        obs["week_idx"],
        obs["last_brand"],
        obs["duration"],
        obs["choice"],
    ]
    return -np.log(np.clip(observed_probs, 1e-300, 1.0)).sum()


def estimate_simple_dynamic_model(obs, price_matrix, d_cap=SIMPLE_D_CAP, delta=SIMPLE_DELTA):
    n_obs = len(obs["choice"])

    starting_values = [
        np.array([2.0, 0.0, np.log(0.05), np.log(0.25), np.log(0.05)]),
        np.array([1.5, 0.5, np.log(0.02), np.log(0.50), np.log(0.10)]),
        np.array([2.5, -0.5, np.log(0.10), np.log(0.10), np.log(0.02)]),
    ]
    bounds = [
        (-10.0, 10.0),
        (-10.0, 10.0),
        (np.log(1e-4), np.log(10.0)),
        (np.log(1e-4), np.log(20.0)),
        (np.log(1e-4), np.log(20.0)),
    ]

    def average_objective(theta_raw):
        return simple_neg_loglik_sum(
            theta_raw,
            obs,
            price_matrix,
            d_cap=d_cap,
            delta=delta,
        ) / n_obs

    results = []
    for start in starting_values:
        result = minimize(
            average_objective,
            start,
            method="L-BFGS-B",
            bounds=bounds,
            options={
                "maxiter": SIMPLE_OPTIMIZER_MAXITER,
                "ftol": 1e-10,
                "gtol": 1e-6,
            },
        )
        results.append(result)

    best_result = min(results, key=lambda res: res.fun)
    return best_result, results


def covariance_from_lbfgs(result, n_obs):
    if not hasattr(result.hess_inv, "todense"):
        return np.full((len(result.x), len(result.x)), np.nan)

    raw_cov = np.asarray(result.hess_inv.todense(), dtype=float) / n_obs
    return raw_cov


def simple_parameter_table(result, model_name, n_obs):
    transformed = unpack_simple_params(result.x)
    raw_cov = covariance_from_lbfgs(result, n_obs)

    rows = [
        ("No-purchase constant", "no_purchase_const", 0, 1.0),
        ("Brand 2 constant", "brand2_const", 1, 1.0),
        ("Price coefficient", "price_coef", 2, transformed["price_coef"]),
        ("Switching cost", "switch_cost", 3, transformed["switch_cost"]),
        ("Duration penalty", "duration_penalty", 4, transformed["duration_penalty"]),
    ]

    table_rows = []
    for parameter_label, parameter_key, raw_idx, derivative in rows:
        estimate = transformed[parameter_key]
        std_err = np.sqrt(max(derivative**2 * raw_cov[raw_idx, raw_idx], 0.0))
        z_stat = estimate / std_err if std_err > 0 else np.nan
        p_value = 2 * (1 - norm.cdf(abs(z_stat))) if np.isfinite(z_stat) else np.nan
        ci_low = estimate - 1.96 * std_err if np.isfinite(std_err) else np.nan
        ci_high = estimate + 1.96 * std_err if np.isfinite(std_err) else np.nan

        table_rows.append(
            {
                "Model": model_name,
                "Parameter": parameter_label,
                "Estimate": estimate,
                "Std. err.": std_err,
                "z-stat": z_stat,
                "p-value": p_value,
                "CI 2.5\\%": ci_low,
                "CI 97.5\\%": ci_high,
            }
        )

    return pd.DataFrame(table_rows)


def simple_fit_table(result, model_name, n_obs, n_params):
    log_likelihood = -result.fun * n_obs
    return {
        "Model": model_name,
        "Converged": "Yes" if result.success else "No",
        "Log-likelihood": log_likelihood,
        "AIC": 2 * n_params - 2 * log_likelihood,
        "BIC": np.log(n_obs) * n_params - 2 * log_likelihood,
        "Iterations": result.nit,
    }


simple_dynamic_result, simple_dynamic_all_results = estimate_simple_dynamic_model(
    simple_obs,
    simple_price_matrix,
    d_cap=SIMPLE_D_CAP,
    delta=SIMPLE_DELTA,
)

simple_myopic_result, simple_myopic_all_results = estimate_simple_dynamic_model(
    simple_obs,
    simple_price_matrix,
    d_cap=SIMPLE_D_CAP,
    delta=0.0,
)

simple_n_obs = len(simple_obs["choice"])
simple_n_params = len(simple_dynamic_result.x)

simple_estimates_table = pd.concat(
    [
        simple_parameter_table(simple_dynamic_result, "Dynamic", simple_n_obs),
        simple_parameter_table(simple_myopic_result, "Myopic", simple_n_obs),
    ],
    ignore_index=True,
)

print_latex_table(
    simple_estimates_table,
    caption="Simple empirical dynamic-logit estimates.",
    label="tbl:simple-dynamic-estimates",
    escape=False,
)

simple_fit_summary = pd.DataFrame(
    [
        simple_fit_table(simple_dynamic_result, "Dynamic", simple_n_obs, simple_n_params),
        simple_fit_table(simple_myopic_result, "Myopic", simple_n_obs, simple_n_params),
    ]
)

print_latex_table(
    simple_fit_summary,
    caption="Fit comparison for the simple empirical model.",
    label="tbl:simple-dynamic-fit",
    escape=True,
)
```
